text: Your Microbiome 02 Overview We craft a microbiome profile, outlining its influence on key health aspects, rooted in the initial bacterial snapshot. Gut Diversity High Overall Health Indicator Dietary Fibre Degraders These are the microbes in the human gut that break down indigestible fibers, producing short-chain fatty acids and other High metabolites that support gut balance, gut barrier and impacts overall health. Energy Producers Some gut bacteria break down complex carbohydrates, fibers, and other substrates into short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs) and High metabolites that provide energy for the host and support other microbes. Immunity The microbiome interacts with the immune system to maintain High gut balance, strengthen the intestinal barrier, and trigger protective responses during infection.

Pathogens & 07 Probiotic Presence Pathogens Some microbes in your gut may be less friendly — but understanding them is the first step toward balance. Bacteria Abundance Level Escherichia-Shigella 0.68% Low Enterobacter 0.64% Low Klebsiella 0.56% Low Stenotrophomonas 0.08% Low Mycoplasma 0.01% Low Legends: Great % Good % Bad % What This Means for You Many pathogens are regular residents of a normal gut. The presence of these pathogens does not necessarily indicate the presence of disease. Any condition will be defined by the nature of the pathogen, your overall health and other conditions. This is not a diagnostic report.

Current: Target 70.92% 75.88% Low Target: 75.88% High Bacteria to Increase Iom boosts your body’s “feel-good” gut bacteria, helping you sleep deeper, feel energized, and stay calm from within. Current: 42.86% Optimised: 53.07% Bacteria to Decrease Iom is decreasing these bacteria to help you unwind better, sleep soundly, and feel lighter each day. Current: 28.06% Optimised: 22.81%

Gut SEnS Bacteria 10 to Decrease Your Current Score Optimised Through Iom Prevotella Blautia Collinsella Fusicatenibacter Coprococcus Other SEnS bacteria to decrease Prevotella Current: 12.44% Optimised: 10.82% Having a Prevotella-rich gut microbiome possibly indicates weight loss, decreases cholesterol levels, and limits the bifidogenic effect in individuals consuming a fibre-rich diet. When in high abundance though, Prevotella is known to cause sleep disturbances. These can make your circadian rhythm(sleep cycle) erratic possibly leaving you tired during the day. Blautia Current: 6.42% Optimised: 5.21% Supposed to have a probiotic effect on the body, Blautia contributes to regulating health by reducing metabolic disorders. Blautia is being decreased to enhance your sleep efficiency and duration while also alleviating the low phases.

Gut SEnS Bacteria 11 to Decrease Collinsella Current: 1.85% Optimised: 1.47% An interesting bacteria commonly seen in the human gut, Collinsella is often a marker for the metabolic condition of the body. The bacteria ferments plant and animal origin carbs to produce lactate that converts to energy. Our feeling of wellness is impacted by our gut and the gut can bring our overall health levels up. To retain its diversity and equilibrium, the Iom optimizer is bringing this bacteria down. Fusicatenibacter Current: 1.59% Optimised: 1.11% A very common gut bacteria, Fusicatenibacter is a happy sugar fermenter with anti inflammatory properties. And in the process of this fermentation they produce the good SCFAs- butyrate and propionate for you. However, this bacteria might be pulling your gut away from peaceful sleep. Iom recommends reducing this bacteria to enhance your sleep quality Coprococcus Current: 1.31% Optimised: 0.92% Coprococcus is a normal resident of your gut flora. These bacteria have anti-inflammatory and anti-bacterial properties. They also work at strengthening the intestinal gut barrier.You might wonder at times why your stress and sleep quality arent as manageable as you would like. Coprococcus abundance negatively impacts both stress and sleep. And Iom optimiser suggests for better SEnS, reduce this one.

Gut SEnS Bacteria 12 to Increase Your Current Score Optimised Through Iom Faecalibacterium Bacteroides Bifidobacterium Dialister Anaerostipes Other SEnS bacteria to increase Faecalibacterium Current: 15.2% Optimised: 18.09% A good bacteria with a prebiotic impact on your gut, Faecalibacterium is an important fella that produces butyrate, and propionate, maintains gut equilibrium, and good gut health. Along with enabling the energy production process, Faecalibacterium are great sleep enhancers. Increasing this one to better your sleep quality and lower any anxiety you deal with. Bacteroides Current: 6.16% Optimised: 7.52% Bacteroides play multiple roles in the gut and keep the system healthy. Contributing to the immune system, they enable expelling toxins and fighting pathogens. These fairly common resident of the gut are being increased for you to ensure better gut health and a movement towards elevated SEnS.

Gut SEnS Bacteria 13 to Increase Bifidobacterium Current: 3.47% Optimised: 4.51% These probiotic, immunity enhancing bacteria live in your intestines and help body fight diarrhea, constipation, and other intestinal disorders. Your performance is defined by your SEnS. Increase your Bifidobacterium to ensure that every game is a win. This bacteria helps your battle with anxiety as well as pushes sleep quality and heightens energy levels. Dialister Current: 3.44% Optimised: 4.47% Dialister is often seen associated with gut imbalance and inflammation. The end products of this fella are usually seen in conditions not best.However, for the SEnS compass, this bacteria adds on to your energy levels and make you more vivacious during the day. The Iom optimiser is marginally increasing this to uplift your vitality. Anaerostipes Current: 2.92% Optimised: 3.8% Anaerostipes -an-earo-stipes are those friends in us who break down plant and fruit sugars and Vitamin B into propionate and acetate-both beneficial to your health. The bacteria aids better balancing glucose at fasting levels.The focus on maximising your every day with appropriate energy levels and keeping depression away can be helped by increasing the levels of Anaerostipes
